top_signals.title: Stocks Selloff Extends on Tech, Oil Rises on Iran: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Stocks extended losses as a selloff in technology shares gathered pace and a robust US jobs data boosted bets on Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes. Oil rose and Treasuries fell as tensions flared in the Middle East.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Saudis Cut July Oil Prices to Asia, Though Still at Decades High
top_signals.summary: Saudi Arabia cut the price of its main crude grade to Asia for a second straight month, though the premium for barrels to the kingdom’s largest market remained near the highest in decades.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Japan’s growth holds up despite drop in business investment
top_signals.summary: The economy grew at a still-solid pace at the start of the year even after the turbulence in Iran prompted businesses to cut spending.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: England’s World Cup opener is set to cause power spike
top_signals.summary: One of the biggest surges is expected at half-time, as hundreds of thousands of viewers leave their couches and switch on their kettles all at once.

top_signals.title: Kimi Antonelli stays cool under fire to win chaotic Monaco Grand Prix
top_signals.summary: Every driver who has won five consecutive races in an F1 season has gone on ⁠to win the title.
